WRITEUP
CAPESIZE
Activity remained subdued after market participants gradually returned from the holiday season. This marked the second publishing day since the Capesize benchmark vessel was changed to 182,000 dwt. However, the downward trend persisted with the 5TC declining by $538 to settle at $27,652 today. The North Atlantic remained quiet, while Brazil/West Africa stems with end-January cancelling were circulated in the market, though no fixtures were reported at the time of writing. Vessels with early February ETAs were said to be attracting limited bidding interest. The C3 route of end January/early February laycan was assessed at 0.064 below $22. In the Pacific, fresh orders from both operators and miners entered the market, but fixture levels fell further compared with last Friday. The C5 route settled at $8.30, down 16 cents from last week. Rio Tinto was reported to have fixed at $8.25 for 170,000/10 Dampier to Qingdao basis 19/21 January.
PANAMAX
Overall, the market had a muted start to the week, which was largely expected on a Monday as most participants returned to their desks following the festive break. Conditions on the fronthaul remain under some pressure, however, the trans Atlantic market appears steadier, having found a more stable trading range. In Asia, the owner sell-off seen toward the end of last year has now largely thinned, with sentiment normalising and owners showing firmer rate ideas, leading to improved numbers being discussed. Cargo volumes remain relatively thin but with the larger and more modern tonnage looking to ballast towards EC South America we may start to experience some higher volatility across the board. The P5TC average posted a $104 increase to publish at $11,640.
Atlantic
Leonidas P.C. (82,165 2011) was fixed aps EC South America 8/10 January for a trip with grains redelivering in Southeast Asia at $15,250 plus a $525,000 ballast bonus. Meanwhile, the Key Future (82,099 2012) was fixed on a retro Immingham 26 December basis for a trip via the US Gulf with minerals redelivering Singapore-Japan at $19,250.
Asia
Messrs SAIL covered their 80,000/10% Coal stem from Gladstone to EC India for 1/10 February dates at $14.05 but further details were limited. The Union Mariner (81,963 2013) open in Qinzhou 6 January was said to have fixed for an Australian round voyage with grains but further details lacked.
SUPRAMAX
A rather slow start to the week as players returned to their desks after the recent holidays and took stock of the upcoming year. The Atlantic remained lethargic with several European Countries looking forward to an additional holiday tomorrow keeping fresh enquiry at a minimum. Elsewhere, both the north and south Atlantic lacked much fresh impetus and rates remained around last done. The Medi Atlantico (60,550 2016) open Abidjan 5/7 January was heard to have recently been on subjects for a trans Atlantic run at $21,000 but no more details came to light. Also, the Xin Hai Tong 51 (56,607 2011) open Cotonou 2/7 January was heard to have been fixed on a fronthaul to the Far East at $17,000, again no more details surfaced. Asia remained negative, brokers saying that there remained an abundance of prompt tonnage and the cargo booked remained relatively restrained. The 11TC average fell $412 from last Friday to start the first full week of the New Year at $13,189.
HANDYSIZE
The first week of the new year opened quietly, with rates in both basins coming under pressure. The BHSI dropped a further 25 points to 660, while the 7TC closed at $11,874. Across the Continent and the Mediterranean, conditions remained weak, as owners either ballasting out of the region or reassessing their expectations downward. Activity in the South Atlantic and the US Gulf was also largely subdued, with freight levels declining across most routes. Charterers continued to push bids lower, meeting resistance from owners and resulting in a widening bid-offer gap. The Asian market opened the week on a quiet note as well, with overall sentiment remaining negative and charterers bidding below previously concluded levels. On the period side, the Ocean Victory (28,386 2011) open Malaysia 13/16 January reported fixed for one year with redelivery worldwide at $10,500, but further details were not disclosed.
All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
Thanks and best regards.
